quick_facts.label: Entry examination
quick_facts.value: Direct recruitment to Sub-Inspector (CBI) is listed through the Staff Selection Commission’s Combined Graduate Level (SSC CGL) examination; applicants must include the post in their SSC preference options to be considered.

quick_facts.label: Post status
quick_facts.value: The SSC CGL 2025 notice lists Sub Inspector, Central Bureau of Investigation as a Group B post in Pay Level 7.

quick_facts.label: Education requirement
quick_facts.value: For this post, the applicable SSC CGL requirement is a bachelor’s degree from a recognized university or equivalent; the notice’s special subject requirements apply only to specified other posts.

quick_facts.label: Age context
quick_facts.value: In the SSC CGL 2025 notice, the CBI Sub-Inspector post carried a 20–30 year age band as of August 1, 2026, subject to the notice’s category-specific relaxations. Age rules can change with a future notification.

quick_facts.label: Post-specific medical standards
quick_facts.value: The cited SSC notice prescribes minimum height, chest (for men), and vision standards for CBI Sub-Inspector candidates; physical and medical checks are conducted by the user department after final selection.

sections.heading: What the role is connected to
paragraphs: A CBI Sub-Inspector is an investigative-law-enforcement entry post within the Central Bureau of Investigation. CBI’s published institutional profile describes a multi-disciplinary central police agency with Anti-Corruption, Economic Offences, Special Crimes, Prosecution, Administration, Policy & Coordination, and forensic functions. In practical terms, the role should be understood as supporting investigations and casework within that wider remit rather than as a general administrative post.

The exact case assignment, powers exercised, and day-to-day mix of fieldwork, records, evidence handling, coordination, and court-related work depend on the unit and investigation. Applicants should avoid assuming a guaranteed specialization merely from selecting the post in SSC CGL.
bullets: Potential investigative domains include corruption-related matters, economic offences, and special-crime work because these are named CBI functional divisions.
The Bureau’s published profile states that it investigates and prosecutes offences across India, so the work context can extend beyond a single local jurisdiction.

sections.heading: Primary entry route: SSC CGL
paragraphs: The canonical direct-entry route is SSC CGL. In the SSC CGL 2025 notice, CBI Sub-Inspector appears under Pay Level 7 with a 20–30 age limit and falls under the general bachelor’s-degree qualification applicable to “all other posts.” These are notification-specific conditions, not permanent guarantees for later cycles.

Selection in that notice used a computer-based Tier I screening examination followed by Tier II. Tier I tested reasoning, general awareness, quantitative aptitude, and English comprehension. Tier II Paper I included mathematical abilities, reasoning and general intelligence, English language and comprehension, general awareness, computer knowledge, and a data-entry speed test. SSC stated that final merit for the relevant posts would be based on Tier II performance, with post preferences submitted online.

Candidates considering CBI Sub-Inspector must assess the physical and medical requirements before indicating the post preference. The notice says that the department conducts those checks after final selection and warns that a candidate who fails them may not then be considered for another post or department.
bullets: Qualification reference: bachelor’s degree from a recognized university or equivalent.
2025-notice age reference: 20–30 years on August 1, 2026; verify the age date and relaxations in the notification actually being applied for.

CBI SI standards in the cited notice: men—165 cm height and 76 cm chest with expansion; women—150 cm height; height relaxation of 5 cm for hillsmen and tribals; specified distant and near vision benchmarks apply.
Post preference matters: SSC stated that candidates who do not indicate a post/department preference are not considered for it.

sections.heading: Skills to build
paragraphs: The SSC CGL examination design makes quantitative reasoning, logical reasoning, English comprehension, general awareness, computer knowledge, and accurate data entry directly relevant preparation skills. These are selection skills; they do not by themselves replace professional investigation training.

For career readiness, it is reasonable to develop careful written communication, document organization, numerical literacy, ethical judgment, and comfort with digital evidence and financial/cybercrime concepts. This is an inference from CBI’s anti-corruption, economic-offences, special-crime, and forensic remit, and from the CBI Academy’s published training coverage in investigation of financial and cyber-enabled crime.
bullets: Prepare SSC CGL foundations: mathematics, reasoning, English, general awareness, computer knowledge, and timed data entry.
Build investigation-adjacent capabilities: precise note-taking and report writing, file and evidence discipline, and basic financial and digital literacy.
Maintain the fitness and vision standards required for the specific CBI SI post notification.

sections.heading: Work setting and training context
paragraphs: CBI is a central-government investigating agency under the Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions, with functions spanning anti-corruption, economic offences, special crimes, prosecution, coordination, administration, and forensic support. Therefore, a Sub-Inspector’s working environment may combine office-based case preparation and coordination with investigation-related duties, depending on the posting and unit.

The CBI Academy in Ghaziabad is identified by the Government of India’s Capacity Building Commission as a central training institution serving CBI officers and serving police officers at Sub-Inspector level and above. Its inclusion indicates an institutional training environment relevant to the profession, though specific induction curriculum and duration should be confirmed from appointment instructions rather than assumed in advance.
bullets: Employer context: Central Bureau of Investigation, under the Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions.
Functional context: anti-corruption, economic-offences, special-crimes, prosecution, policy/coordination, administration, and forensic functions.
Training-institution context: CBI Academy, Ghaziabad, Uttar Pradesh.

sections.heading: Pay context
paragraphs: Salary should be treated as dated, rule-based context rather than a take-home-pay promise. The SSC CGL 2025 notice placed CBI Sub-Inspector in Pay Level 7 and showed the Level 7 pay-matrix range as ₹44,900–₹1,42,400. Actual monthly pay varies with admissible allowances, place of posting, deductions, and any later government pay revisions.

The same SSC material classified the role as Group B; a 2025 SSC tentative-vacancy document further described the CBI SI listing as Group B (Non-Gazetted). Vacancy counts and compensation components vary by recruitment cycle and should be checked in the live SSC notice and relevant government orders.
bullets: Dated basic-pay context from SSC CGL 2025: Pay Level 7, ₹44,900–₹1,42,400.
Do not equate the pay-matrix range with in-hand salary; allowances and deductions are variable.
The 2025 tentative-vacancy document listed 93 CBI SI vacancies as of July 31, 2025; this is historical context only and not a forecast of future vacancies.
